idea: Reusable GitHub Actions pipeline with OIDC-based cloud deploys
why_this_project_matters: This project demonstrates a more mature delivery design where build logic is reused, permissions are short-lived, and promotion controls are explicit.
architecture: A reusable build workflow handles quality checks and image publishing. A top-level pipeline orchestrates staging and production jobs, assumes cloud roles through GitHub OIDC, and keeps deployment permissions separated by environment.
steps: Create cloud roles that trust GitHub OIDC for the repository and environment you intend to deploy from.
Wire repository variables for role ARNs and registry locations rather than hardcoding them in workflow files.
Use the reusable build workflow from multiple services to reduce duplication and standardize checks.
Protect production with environment approvals and a separate deployment job.
Audit workflow permissions regularly and remove scopes the pipeline no longer needs.
